name: An accurate, efficient, and accessible AI-powered solution for wildlife re-identification in conservation
impact_statement: Lets conservationists re-identify individual giraffes and other wildlife across repeat surveys far faster and cheaper, supporting population monitoring of endangered species.
summary: GIRAFFE (Generalized Image-based Re-identification using AI for Fauna Feature Extraction) is an AI system that automates re-identification of individual giraffes from field photos using local feature matching, with a user interface that lets technical and non-technical users curate large datasets and analyze repeat surveys. Validated on real Masai giraffe datasets, it exceeds 0.9 accuracy across nine standard metrics, runs roughly 120 times faster than baseline methods with a 132-fold cost improvement, and is designed to extend to other species.

name: Fields of The World: A Machine Learning Benchmark Dataset for Global Agricultural Field Boundary Segmentation
impact_statement: Gives agricultural monitoring a large, openly available benchmark for automatically mapping crop field boundaries from satellite imagery across 24 countries, enabling scalable food security and land use assessments.
summary: Fields of The World (FTW) is a benchmark dataset for agricultural field instance segmentation spanning 24 countries across four continents, with 70,462 samples pairing instance and semantic segmentation masks with multi-date, multi-spectral Sentinel-2 imagery. It is an order of magnitude larger than prior datasets and ships baseline models that show stronger zero-shot and fine-tuning performance in held-out countries.

name: Forecasting acute childhood malnutrition in Kenya using machine learning and diverse sets of indicators
impact_statement: Helps Kenyan health decision-makers anticipate where acute child malnutrition will rise across sub-counties so interventions can be targeted before crises escalate.
summary: A spatio-temporal machine learning framework that forecasts acute malnutrition risk among under-5 children for each sub-county in Kenya. It combines routine clinical indicators from the national DHIS2 health system with satellite-derived signals such as gross primary productivity to predict moderate, severe, and aggregated cases across multiple time horizons.

name: Global Renewables Watch: A Temporal Dataset of Solar and Wind Energy Derived from Satellite Imagery
impact_statement: Maps where and when the world builds solar and wind power so policymakers and researchers can measure and accelerate progress toward clean energy and sustainable development goals.
summary: Global Renewables Watch is a global, quarterly temporal dataset of commercial solar PV farms and onshore wind turbines derived from high-resolution satellite imagery spanning Q4 2017 to Q2 2024. Deep learning segmentation models were run across more than 13 trillion pixels to detect 375,197 wind turbines and 86,410 solar PV installations, each tagged with an estimated construction date and preceding land use. Country-level capacity estimates aggregated from these detections match IRENA's 2023 figures with r2 of 0.96 for solar PV and 0.93 for onshore wind.

name: Mapping global floods with 10 years of satellite radar data
impact_statement: Delivers a decade-long, cloud-free global map of flood extent that pinpoints historically flood-prone communities and supports real-time disaster response.
summary: A deep learning model detects flood extent from Sentinel-1 radar imagery, which penetrates clouds and works day or night, applied to 10 years of global SAR data. The resulting longitudinal dataset identifies historically flood-prone areas, was used to flag vulnerable communities in Ethiopia, supported real-time response to the May 2024 Kenya floods, and suggests a possible upward trend in global flood extent.

name: Project SPARROW and the Future of Conservation Technology
impact_statement: Protects fragile remote ecosystems by enabling continuous, autonomous wildlife monitoring where power and connectivity are scarce, streaming real-time conservation insights to the people who safeguard them
summary: SPARROW is an open-source hardware and software platform that combines solar energy, edge AI, and satellite communication for continuous, autonomous biodiversity monitoring in remote environments. Each node runs on-device deep learning over visual, acoustic, and environmental sensors and transmits summarized results over Low-Earth-Orbit satellite or GSM networks. Deployed across tropical, temperate, and montane ecosystems in Colombia, Peru, Tanzania, and the United States, it sustained 24/7 operation and collected more than two million images and acoustic recordings in its first 190 days.

name: Rapid building damage assessment workflow: An implementation for the 2023 Rolling Fork, Mississippi tornado event
impact_statement: Gives disaster first responders accurate building damage maps within two hours of a satellite pass so relief efforts can be targeted where they are most needed.
summary: A human-in-the-loop workflow for rapidly training building damage assessment models from high-resolution satellite imagery after a natural disaster. Applied with the American Red Cross to the March 2023 tornado in Rolling Fork, Mississippi, it reached 0.86 precision and 0.80 recall on damaged buildings while running end-to-end in under two hours per imagery scene.

name: TEMPO: Global Temporal Building Density and Height Estimation from Satellite Imagery
impact_statement: Delivers quarterly global maps of building density and height so planners and researchers can track where settlements are growing and inform climate resilience and adaptation efforts.
summary: TEMPO is a global, temporally resolved dataset of building density and height derived from quarterly PlanetScope satellite basemaps using a multi-task deep learning model at 37.6-meter resolution. Covering Q1 2018 through Q2 2025, it captures quarterly changes in built settlements with F1 scores of 85 to 88 percent and strong temporal stability, at a fraction of the compute cost of comparable approaches.

name: TorchGeo: Deep Learning With Geospatial Data
impact_statement: Makes it practical to apply deep learning to satellite and remote sensing imagery, powering work from precision agriculture and disaster monitoring to climate change research.
summary: TorchGeo is an open-source Python library that brings geospatial data into the PyTorch deep learning ecosystem. It provides data loaders for benchmark and uncurated remote sensing datasets, geospatial samplers, multispectral transforms, and pre-trained models, handling the coordinate systems, resolutions, and extra spectral bands that make deep learning on satellite imagery hard.

name: Acoustic detection of regionally rare bird species through deep convolutional neural networks
impact_statement: Lets conservationists monitor the activity and abundance of regionally rare Himalayan bird species from passive field audio recordings to guide habitat protection in Nepal.
summary: This work trains deep convolutional neural networks on passive field audio recordings to detect the calls of two regionally rare bird species, the Yellow-vented warbler and Rufous-throated wren-babbler, along an elevational transect in Nepal's Barun River Valley. Combined with data augmentation and expert labeling, the classifier reveals each species' activity, abundance, and habitat preferences across ecosystems to support conservation decision-making.

name: Adaptive Acoustic Monitoring for Endangered Cook Inlet Beluga Whales in Complex Soundscapes
impact_statement: Enables broader and more reliable acoustic detection of endangered Cook Inlet beluga whales across noisy, underrepresented habitats to strengthen their conservation.
summary: An open-source deep learning framework for passive acoustic monitoring of the endangered Cook Inlet beluga whale. It uses a two-stage model that separates signal detection from species classification, extends coverage to killer whales and humpback whales, and combines contrastive audio-language models with active learning to improve robustness across variable, noisy soundscapes.

name: AI Diffusion in Low Resource Language Countries
impact_statement: Shows that limited language support in AI systems is an independent barrier to adoption, with low-resource-language countries having about 20 percent fewer AI users, giving policymakers a measurable target for closing the global AI divide.
summary: A cross-country study that uses a weighted regression model to separate the effect of language from socioeconomic and demographic factors on AI adoption. It finds that low-resource language countries have roughly 20 percent fewer AI users than their baseline would predict, indicating that linguistic accessibility is a significant, independent barrier to equitable AI diffusion.

name: AI-Driven Detection and Analysis of Handwriting on Seized Ivory
impact_statement: Turns handwritten markings on seized elephant tusks into a low-cost forensic signal that helps investigators link ivory shipments and disrupt trafficking networks
summary: An AI pipeline detects, extracts, and describes handwritten markings on photographs of seized elephant tusks, then identifies recurring signature markings that connect shipments across different seizures. Built from 6,085 photographs of eight large ivory seizures collected over 2014-2019, it yielded more than 17,000 individual markings and 184 recurring signature markings, offering a scalable, low-cost complement to expensive DNA-based ivory forensics.

name: AI-Enabled Screening for Retinopathy of Prematurity in Low-Resource Settings
impact_statement: Enables early detection of retinopathy of prematurity from smartphone-recorded retinal videos so that preventable childhood blindness can be caught and treated where pediatric eye specialists and imaging cameras are scarce.
summary: A diagnostic study that developed a machine learning system to screen premature newborns for retinopathy of prematurity (ROP), the leading cause of preventable childhood blindness. The algorithm processes smartphone-captured fundus videos, selects the best frames, and determines whether ROP is likely, targeting neonatal care in low-resource settings. It was developed and evaluated using videos from premature neonates in Mexico and Argentina.

name: Reimagining leprosy elimination with AI analysis of skin lesion images with demographic and clinical data
impact_statement: Speeds leprosy diagnosis in underserved communities by letting health workers screen skin lesions at over 90% accuracy, helping prevent irreversible nerve damage and curb ongoing transmission.
summary: AI4Leprosy is an AI-enabled diagnostic assistant that fuses smartphone skin lesion images with standardized demographic and clinical data to estimate the probability of leprosy. It was built and evaluated on 1229 images from 222 patients at a Brazilian national referral center, reaching over 90% accuracy when image and clinical features were combined, so clinicians in remote settings can flag suspected cases sooner.

name: An Artificial Intelligence Dataset for Solar Energy Locations in India
impact_statement: Maps 1,363 utility-scale solar farms across India and reveals that over 74 percent were built on ecologically or agriculturally valuable land, giving planners the evidence to site future solar capacity without displacing natural ecosystems.
summary: A machine learning model maps utility-scale solar photovoltaic installations across India from freely available satellite imagery at 92 percent mean accuracy, producing an expert-validated open dataset of 1,363 solar PV farms. The work measures India's solar footprint and quantifies the landcover it has modified, supporting land use planning as the country pursues 500 gigawatts of solar capacity by 2030.

name: An Analysis of the Deployment of Models Trained on Private Tabular Synthetic Data: Unexpected Surprises
impact_statement: Shows that machine learning models built and tested on differentially private synthetic data can misjudge their real-world accuracy and bias, helping teams share sensitive data privately without unknowingly deploying unreliable or unfair models.
summary: A systematic study of how differentially private synthetic tabular data affects downstream classification, measuring both utility and algorithmic fairness. It finds that stronger privacy reduces accuracy but does not necessarily increase bias, and that evaluating models on synthetic data can misestimate their true performance on real data, motivating stricter testing protocols for privacy-preserving pipelines.

name: Towards an automated Segment Anything Model (SAM) for lesion segmentation in oncological PET/CT images
impact_statement: Measures how well tumor segmentation models transfer between cancer types, pointing toward PET/CT lesion detection that generalizes without type-specific labeled data.
summary: This work trains Residual-UNet deep learning models to segment tumor lesions in PET/CT scans of a single cancer type (lymphoma, lung cancer, or melanoma) and tests how well that segmentation ability transfers to other cancer types. It evaluates ensembling techniques such as averaging, voting, and STAPLE across five cross-validation models as a route to improving generalizability to new cancer types.

name: Automatic segmentation of prostate cancer metastases in PSMA PET/CT images with weighted batch-wise dice loss
impact_statement: Enables fully automated detection and segmentation of metastatic prostate cancer lesions in whole-body PSMA PET/CT scans to support personalized radiopharmaceutical therapy and treatment response monitoring.
summary: A U-Net based convolutional neural network framework detects and segments metastatic prostate cancer lesions in whole-body PSMA PET/CT images. A proposed weighted batch-wise dice loss and the inclusion of neighboring axial slices improve detection of small and low-intensity lesions, with the final model detecting about 80 percent of test lesions overall and 93 percent of higher-uptake lesions.

name: BankNote-Net: Open dataset for assistive universal currency recognition
impact_statement: Enables assistive apps to recognize banknotes across 17 currencies and 112 denominations so people who are blind or have low vision can identify money independently.
summary: BankNote-Net is an open dataset of 24,826 compliant banknote-image embeddings spanning 17 currencies and 112 denominations, collected in real assistive settings. Using supervised contrastive learning, the authors train a universal currency-recognition encoder whose shareable embeddings support few-shot downstream models for currencies not in the dataset, and a variant of the model was deployed in Microsoft's Seeing AI app.

name: Becoming Good at AI for Good
impact_statement: Distills eleven practical takeaways that help future AI for good collaborations deliver real-world impact across sustainability, health, humanitarian aid, and social justice.
summary: An experience-based paper on how to run AI for Good projects effectively in collaboration with domain-expert partners. It organizes lessons into four categories (communication, data, modeling, and impact), distills eleven takeaways to guide future projects, and illustrates them with two case studies from past collaborations.

name: Beluga whale acoustic signal classification using deep learning neural network models
impact_statement: Automatically flagging true beluga calls in passive acoustic recordings speeds up monitoring of an endangered Cook Inlet population so conservation managers can track its seasonal occurrence and respond to threats.
summary: This work builds an ensembled convolutional neural network that classifies detections from passive acoustic moorings as true or false beluga whale signals. Trained on NOAA monitoring data from Alaska's Cook Inlet, the model reaches 96.57% precision and 92.26% recall, replacing time-intensive manual validation and generalizing to other acoustic classification problems.

name: Benchmarking robustness of automated CT pancreas segmentation
impact_statement: Measures whether automated CT pancreas segmentation reaches human-level reliability so that CT based early cancer detection and quantitative biomarker tools can be trusted in clinical deployment.
summary: This work systematically evaluates the robustness of deep learning models for pancreas segmentation on CT, defining robustness as the share of cases that reach human-level performance rather than relying on mean overlap metrics. Using 903 venous-phase CT scans and a 100-case healthy test set each segmented by four independent readers, it introduces a Fractional Threshold metric and a human-in-the-loop active learning strategy to improve reliability toward the empirical human distribution.

name: Binary Mode Multinomial Deep Learning Model for more efficient Automated Diabetic Retinopathy Detection
impact_statement: Helps clinicians catch diabetic eye disease earlier by accurately flagging which patients need referral from color fundus photographs.
summary: This work compares binary referral classification (Refer/No Refer) against multinomial severity classification of diabetic retinopathy from color fundus photographs using deep learning. Running a multinomial model in "binary mode" with image-processing annotations reached up to 97.69% accuracy on clinical datasets, matching or exceeding simple binary classification while enabling earlier assessment of diabetic eye disease.

name: A Biologist's Guide to the Galaxy: Leveraging Artificial Intelligence and Very High-Resolution Satellite Imagery to Monitor Marine Mammals from Space
impact_statement: Advances a satellite and AI platform that lets agencies and scientists locate endangered whales across remote oceans, filling gaps in marine mammal distribution to support conservation of at-risk species.
summary: A vision and lessons-learned paper from the Geospatial Artificial Intelligence for Animals (GAIA) initiative, which combines very high-resolution satellite imagery, cloud computing, and machine learning to detect cetaceans from space. It describes the challenges, dataset-creation approaches, and roadmap for an operational platform to monitor marine mammal distribution, with a focus on endangered species like the North Atlantic right whale and Cook Inlet beluga.

name: A pipeline for identification of bird and frog species in tropical soundscape recordings using a convolutional neural network
impact_statement: Enables large-scale acoustic monitoring of tropical biodiversity by automatically identifying bird and frog species from continuous soundscape recordings.
summary: An end-to-end deep learning pipeline that trains a convolutional neural network on mel-spectrograms to detect multiple bird and frog species in raw, unlabeled soundscape recordings. Training data are gathered semi-automatically with a template-based signal detection algorithm and a validation interface, and the method was evaluated on recordings from 749 sites in Puerto Rico to identify 24 regional bird and frog species.

name: Bootstrapping Rare Object Detection in High-Resolution Satellite Imagery
impact_statement: Lets conservation teams map hard-to-find livestock enclosures across the Serengeti Mara from satellite imagery without any starting labels, making landscape monitoring feasible on minimal annotation budgets.
summary: The work introduces offline and online cluster-based sampling methods that surface rare objects to human annotators far more efficiently than random search, so useful detectors can be bootstrapped from scratch with almost no labeled data and no spatial prior. Applied to detecting bomas, small enclosures for herd animals, in the Serengeti Mara region of Kenya and Tanzania, it raised the positive sampling rate from 2 percent to 30 percent and reached an F1 of 0.51 with a budget of just 300 labeled patches.

name: The Risk of Hospitalization and Mortality After Breakthrough SARS-CoV-2 Infection by Vaccine Type
impact_statement: Measures how the risk of hospitalization and death after a breakthrough COVID-19 infection differs by vaccine brand, age, and prior infection so public health officials can better target vaccination and booster efforts.
summary: An observational study of medical claims data from 19,815 fully vaccinated, SARS-CoV-2 positive patients that uses Cox regression to compare the risk of hospitalization and mortality across the Pfizer, Moderna, and Janssen vaccines while controlling for age, sex, comorbidities, and prior infection. It found lower severe-outcome hazard rates for Moderna and Pfizer recipients than for Janssen recipients.

name: Bridging gaps in ophthalmology education through large language models
impact_statement: Measures how reliably general-purpose language models answer ophthalmology board questions, showing reasoning models can match or exceed residents and could serve as trustworthy study aids for eye-care trainees.
summary: This study evaluates OpenAI GPT models (GPT-4o, GPT-4-Turbo, o1, o3-mini, and GPT-4.1) on 3,357 questions from the American Academy of Ophthalmology Self-Assessment Program, using zero-shot and chain-of-thought prompting plus multimodal analysis for image-based questions. It compares the models against 1,371 ophthalmology residents, finding that o1 with chain-of-thought prompting was the most accurate model and outperformed residents on several question types.

name: Cancer Detection in Breast MRI Screening via Explainable AI Anomaly Detection
impact_statement: Helps radiologists catch breast cancers earlier by flagging suspicious screening MRI scans and highlighting the tissue regions that drive each prediction.
summary: An explainable AI anomaly detection model that learns from mostly normal breast MRI scans and identifies cancers as deviations from healthy tissue. It surpassed conventional binary classifiers in both high- and low-cancer-prevalence scenarios and produced explanation heat maps that were not significantly different from radiologist annotations.

name: Capturing COVID-19-Like Symptoms at Scale Using Banner Ads on an Online News Platform
impact_statement: Shows that low-cost banner ads on a news platform can recruit tens of thousands of people worldwide to self-report COVID-19 symptoms, enabling rapid population-level disease surveillance when testing is scarce.
summary: A pilot study that recruited volunteers through banner ads on Microsoft News to complete a short online epidemiologic survey about COVID-19 symptoms, testing, and travel. Over 87,000 respondents across the United States, Japan, and other countries completed it in three weeks, and 22.3% reported at least one COVID-19-like symptom. The work demonstrates that online news platforms are a feasible channel for large-scale, low-cost symptom surveillance during a pandemic.

name: Caribou Aerial Patches — OWL Benchmark Dataset
impact_statement: Enables reproducible, cross-herd evaluation of automated caribou counting from aerial survey imagery so wildlife managers can monitor Arctic herds without retraining detectors for every new survey.
summary: A point-annotated benchmark of 512x512 aerial image patches for detecting and counting caribou in overhead survey imagery, with training data from the Porcupine Caribou Herd (Alaska, 2017) and a strict cross-herd, cross-temporal test set from the Central Arctic Herd (2022). It ships pre-trained HerdNet weights that reproduce an F1 of 0.965 and supports evaluating point-based object detectors that generalize across herds and years without per-deployment retraining.

name: Parameters of Chronic Kidney Disease to Identify Outpatients at Increased Risk for COVID-19 Mortality: A Cohort Study of UK Biobank Participants
impact_statement: Pinpoints which chronic kidney disease measures flag outpatients at higher risk of dying from COVID-19, helping clinicians and policymakers prioritize protection and vaccination for the most vulnerable.
summary: A retrospective cohort study of more than 500,000 UK Biobank participants that mined outpatient primary-care data, with a focus on chronic kidney disease parameters, to identify which pre-morbid clinical factors are associated with COVID-19 mortality. The analysis controlled for confounders and aimed to inform how high-risk outpatients are prioritized for vaccination and care.

name: A comparison of convolutional neural networks and few-shot learning in classifying long-tailed distributed tropical bird songs
impact_statement: Improves acoustic identification of both common and rare tropical bird species in remote rainforests, strengthening biodiversity monitoring where annotated call data is scarce.
summary: This work compares convolutional neural networks with a Siamese few-shot learning approach for classifying bird songs in a highly imbalanced, long-tailed tropical soundscape. Using over 600 hours of audio and manually annotated calls from 101 bird species on Barro Colorado Island, Panama, it evaluates which method more accurately identifies both common species and the many rare species with few training examples.

name: Comparing recurrent convolutional neural networks for large scale bird species classification
impact_statement: Enables automated identification of 100 bird species from audio recordings, supporting large scale acoustic monitoring of avian biodiversity.
summary: A deep learning study that classifies 100 bird species from spectrograms of field audio recordings in the Cornell Bird Challenge 2020 dataset. It compares stand-alone CNNs against hybrid CNN-RNN models (LSTM, GRU, and Legendre Memory Units), finding that a CNN-RNN hybrid is most accurate, reaching 67% average accuracy across species.

name: Computer-Aided Detection of Small Metastatic Prostate Cancer Lesions on PSMA PET Using Multi-Angle Maximum Intensity Projections
impact_statement: Detects small, easily missed metastatic lesions in men with recurrent prostate cancer from PSMA PET scans, supporting earlier and more targeted treatment decisions.
summary: This work builds a computer-aided detection method that flags small metastatic lesions in patients with biochemically recurrent prostate cancer directly from PSMA PET scans. It runs object detection models on multi-angle maximum intensity projections of the 3D volumes, back-projects the 2D detections into 3D, and segments the lesions with a fine-tuned MedSAM. The best model reached an F1-score of 0.69 with strong recall for local relapses and bone metastases, matching 3D methods while being more efficient.

name: Core-Set Selection for Data-efficient Land Cover Segmentation
impact_statement: Enables accurate land cover mapping from remote sensing imagery while cutting the training data and compute needed by up to 75 percent.
summary: Introduces and benchmarks six core-set selection methods that pick high-quality subsets of remote sensing training data for land cover semantic segmentation. Across three land-cover datasets (DFC2022, Vaihingen, Potsdam) and two architectures (SegFormer and U-Net), selecting as little as 25 percent of the data matches or exceeds performance using the full dataset, establishing a baseline for data-centric Earth observation.

name: Deep learning models for COVID-19 chest x-ray classification: Preventing shortcut learning using feature disentanglement
impact_statement: Makes COVID-19 diagnosis from chest x-rays more trustworthy by stopping models from latching onto dataset artifacts, so predictions hold up on patients and scanners the model has never seen.
summary: This work adds feature disentanglement to the training of deep learning models that classify COVID-19 from chest radiographs, penalizing the network for learning features that reveal which source dataset an image came from. The technique reduces shortcut learning and improves generalization to unseen data, raising AUC by up to 0.13, and outperforms masking out non-lung regions combined with histogram equalization.

name: COVID-19 Pandemic-Era Changes in Sudden Unexpected Infant Death in the United States
impact_statement: Shows that sudden unexpected infant death rates rose after the onset of the COVID-19 pandemic and hit infants of Black, younger, and lower-income mothers hardest, pointing prevention efforts toward the families most at risk.
summary: A nationwide cohort study of US births from 2016 to 2021 using CDC National Center for Health Statistics data, measuring how postperinatal sudden unexpected infant death (SUID) rates changed after the onset of the COVID-19 pandemic. It found overall SUID rates increased from 87.6 to 95.3 per 100,000 births, with the largest increases among infants born to non-Hispanic Black mothers, younger mothers, mothers with lower educational attainment, and Medicaid recipients.

name: Current engagement with unreliable sites from web search driven by navigational search
impact_statement: Measures how much web search actually exposes users to unreliable information sources, showing that most such engagement comes from users deliberately seeking those sites rather than from the search algorithm.
summary: An analysis of roughly 14 billion Bing search engine result pages finds that web search exposes users to very few unreliable information sources. The vast majority of engagement with unreliable sites occurs when users explicitly search for those sites by name, underscoring the role of user preference over search-engine algorithms in driving exposure to misinformation.

name: Analyzing Decades-Long Environmental Changes in Namibia Using Archival Aerial Photography and Deep Learning
impact_statement: Measures how waterholes, homesteads, and big trees around Oshikango, Namibia shifted between 1943 and 1972, turning decades-old aerial photographs into a quantified record of postwar environmental and land-use change.
summary: This work builds a deep semantic segmentation workflow that detects waterholes, Omuti homesteads, and big trees in sub-meter grayscale aerial photographs of Oshikango, Namibia from 1943 and 1972. Using class weighting, pseudo-labeling, and empirical p-value filtering to cope with sparse hand labels, it reconstructs how the size and distribution of these features changed over nearly three decades, reflecting the local effects of massive post-Second World War economic, agricultural, and demographic shifts.

name: DeepDeg: Forecasting and explaining degradation in novel photovoltaics
impact_statement: Speeds the development of longer-lasting next-generation solar cells by forecasting and explaining how they degrade from only a few hours of testing, cutting stability screening time 5 to 20 fold.
summary: DeepDeg is a machine learning model that combines deep learning, explainable machine learning, and physical modeling to forecast hundreds of hours of degradation in novel photovoltaics and identify the physiochemical factors driving it. Trained on over 785 stability tests of organic solar cells totaling 230,000 measurement hours, it predicts degradation dynamics from a few initial hours of measurement, accelerating stability characterization and screening by 5 to 20 times.

name: Democratizing protein language models with parameter-efficient fine-tuning
impact_statement: Lets research groups with limited computing resources adapt large protein language models to predict protein interactions and structural symmetry using far less memory and orders of magnitude fewer parameters.
summary: This work brings parameter-efficient fine-tuning (using LoRA) from natural language processing to proteomics, adapting large protein language models for protein-protein interaction prediction and homooligomer quaternary-structure symmetry prediction. The approach matches full fine-tuning while cutting memory use and trainable parameters dramatically, lowering the barrier for resource-constrained labs.

name: On the Deployment of Post-Disaster Building Damage Assessment Tools using Satellite Imagery: A Deep Learning Approach
impact_statement: Speeds post-disaster damage mapping so humanitarian responders can direct shelter, medical aid, and food to the hardest-hit communities within hours of a disaster.
summary: A deep learning system that uses high-resolution before-and-after satellite imagery to localize buildings and score their damage on the four-level xView2 scale. Developed with the Netherlands Red Cross 510 initiative, it prioritizes inference speed for emergency response, running roughly three times faster than the fastest xView2 challenge winning solution while reaching a pixel-wise F1 of 0.74 for building localization.

name: Detecting Cattle and Elk in the Wild from Space
impact_statement: Lets ecologists count and locate cattle and elk across large landscapes directly from satellite imagery, enabling wildlife and rangeland monitoring without costly field surveys.
summary: This work introduces CowNet, a deep learning baseline that simultaneously counts large ungulates like cows and elk and localizes them at the pixel level in sub-meter satellite imagery, along with an evaluation methodology that accounts for noisy labels and the needs of ecological monitoring stakeholders. Benchmarked against state-of-the-art object-counting methods over Point Reyes Seashore, CA, the LC-FCN model performed best, reaching average precision of 0.56 to 0.61 and average recall of 0.78 to 0.92 across held-out test scenes.

name: Detecting, classifying, and counting blue whale calls with Siamese neural networks
impact_statement: Automatically detects, classifies, and counts calls from four acoustic blue whale populations in ocean recordings so each population's conservation status can be better assessed.
summary: This work builds a deep learning model that detects, classifies, and counts blue whale calls across four acoustic song types using 350 hours of annotated hydrophone recordings from the Indian Ocean. It uses Siamese neural networks, which compare feature vectors to measure similarity, and shows they outperform conventional convolutional neural networks on both population classification and call-count estimation.

name: Assessment of differentially private synthetic data for utility and fairness in end-to-end machine learning pipelines for tabular data
impact_statement: Shows how to share sensitive tabular data as privacy-preserving synthetic datasets without sacrificing model accuracy or fairness, so regulated fields like health care and humanitarian aid can build machine learning models on data that could not otherwise be released.
summary: This study systematically evaluates whether differentially private synthetic tabular data can replace real data across full machine learning pipelines, comparing marginal-based and GAN-based generation methods. It measures both downstream classification utility and fairness, and introduces a training and evaluation framework that does not assume real data is available for testing.

name: Distribution Shifts at Scale: Out-of-distribution Detection in Earth Observation
impact_statement: Flags satellite inputs that fall outside a model's training distribution so globally deployed Earth observation systems stay trustworthy in low-data regions.
summary: TARDIS is a post-hoc out-of-distribution detection method for geospatial deep learning models deployed at global scale. It generates surrogate distribution labels from in-distribution features and trains a binary classifier to identify inputs that deviate from the training distribution, validated on EuroSAT, xBD, and Fields of the World across 17 covariate and semantic shift setups without degrading the model's primary task.

name: Dwelling Type Classification for Disaster Risk Assessment Using Satellite Imagery
impact_statement: Maps dwelling types from satellite imagery so at-risk homes in India's low-income settlements can be scored and prioritized for protection before floods and other hazards strike.
summary: This work automates hyper-local disaster vulnerability assessment by classifying seven dwelling types from satellite imagery of low-income and vulnerable settlements in India, framed as a semantic segmentation task using a TernausNet (U-Net) model. The predicted dwelling types feed a risk-scoring model combined with regional inundation data, and the pipeline was deployed ahead of 2020 natural hazards and validated against post-hoc ground-truth.

name: Effective deep learning approaches for predicting COVID-19 outcomes from chest computed tomography volumes
impact_statement: Helps clinicians distinguish COVID-19 pneumonia from other lung conditions and predict patient mortality by combining chest CT scans with clinical data.
summary: This work develops deep learning methods that aggregate 3D chest CT volumes into a 2D patient-level representation combinable with clinical metadata to separate COVID-19 pneumonia from healthy lungs and other viral pneumonia. It also introduces a multitask model that jointly segments multiple classes of pulmonary lesions in infected lungs and compares these approaches for predicting mortality.

name: Energy Use of AI Inference: Efficiency Pathways and Test-Time Compute
impact_statement: Grounds datacenter capacity planning, efficiency investment, and energy policy in production-realistic per-query energy and water estimates for large-scale AI inference, replacing figures overstated by 4 to 20 times.
summary: A bottom-up framework estimates the energy used by large-scale AI inference from token throughput, node power, and overhead under production deployment assumptions. For frontier-scale models on H100 nodes it finds a median of 0.31 Wh per query, rising 13 times under test-time scaling, and identifies 8 to 20 times line-of-sight efficiency reductions, with datacenter-scale energy and water demand estimated at the billion-query-per-day level.

name: Enhancing Food Security With High-Quality Land-Use and Land-Cover Maps: A Local Model Approach
impact_statement: Delivers accurate local land-use and land-cover maps for African agricultural regions to support the resource management and planning that strengthens food security.
summary: A data-centric teacher-student framework that produces high-quality local land-cover maps for regions where global maps are inaccurate due to sparse training data. A high-resolution teacher model trained on 0.331 m/pixel satellite imagery distills its outputs as weak labels into a low-resolution student model trained on freely available 10 m/pixel imagery, evaluated over Murang'a county, Kenya, a highly agricultural region.

name: Estimating Vaccine-Preventable COVID-19 Deaths Under Counterfactual Vaccination Scenarios in the United States
impact_statement: Quantifies for every US state how many COVID-19 deaths could have been averted with higher vaccination coverage, giving public health leaders evidence to target vaccination efforts.
summary: A counterfactual simulation study that estimates a lower bound on vaccine-preventable COVID-19 deaths for each US state under 85%, 90%, and 100% adult vaccination scenarios. It combines state-level demographic data with county-level vaccination statistics to model how many SARS-CoV-2 deaths among unvaccinated people might have been prevented, finding roughly 319,000 deaths (about half of reported COVID-19 deaths in the period) could have been avoided under full coverage.

name: Performance of explainable artificial intelligence in guiding the management of patients with a pancreatic cyst
impact_statement: Helps clinicians decide whether a patient with a pancreatic cyst should be discharged, monitored, or sent to surgery, cutting unnecessary operations while catching cancers earlier.
summary: This work develops explainable boosting machine models that stratify pancreatic cyst patients into discharge, monitoring, or surgery pathways based on malignancy risk. Using a public dataset of 850 cases, models built from clinical features alone or combined with cyst fluid molecular markers outperformed current clinical care and would have reduced unnecessary surgical resections.

name: Fast building segmentation from satellite imagery and few local labels
impact_statement: Maps building footprints in rapidly urbanizing cities from only a few hundred local labels, giving planners current data on growth and post-conflict recovery.
summary: A method for training a local building-footprint segmentation model on a single high-resolution (0.5m/px) satellite scene using very few sparse polygon labels, avoiding the domain-shift problems of models transferred across regions. The best model, trained on just 527 sparse annotations, reached 0.87 recall on held-out footprints, and the approach was applied to study urban change in Amman, Jordan.

name: Reducing bias and increasing utility by federated generative modeling of medical images using a centralized adversary
impact_statement: Lets sites with limited, biased medical imaging data collaboratively produce higher-quality, less-biased synthetic images without any of them exposing their patients' raw data.
summary: FELICIA (Federated Learning with a Centralized Adversary) is a generative mechanism that extends the local PrivGAN approach to federated, non-IID settings across a family of GAN architectures. It shows that a site holding limited and biased medical images can benefit from other sites to generate high-utility synthetic images while every site keeps its own data private, sharing only through a central discriminator.

name: Improved estimation of the relationship between fetal growth and late stillbirth in the United States, 2014-15
impact_statement: It reveals how strongly a baby's growth predicts late stillbirth risk across the full birthweight range, so fetuses at the extreme centiles can be identified and monitored earlier.
summary: Using US CDC live birth and fetal death records for singleton births from 2014 to 2015, this study models late stillbirth risk against customized (GROW) birthweight centiles. It shows that the standard small- and large-for-gestational-age cutoffs fit poorly and badly underestimate risk at the extremes, and that a continuous generalized additive model captures the birthweight-stillbirth relationship far more accurately.

name: FLAVARS: A Multimodal Foundational Language and Vision Alignment Model for Remote Sensing
impact_statement: Enables a single satellite-imagery model to handle segmentation, classification, and zero-shot recognition well, so earth-observation teams no longer trade vision accuracy for language capability.
summary: FLAVARS is a pretraining method for remote sensing imagery that unites contrastive image-text learning, masked image modeling, and geospatial alignment through contrastive location encoding. It significantly outperforms the SkyCLIP baseline on vision-only tasks such as KNN classification and semantic segmentation (+6% mIOU on SpaceNet1) while retaining zero-shot classification that masked-autoencoder methods lack.

name: Food security analysis and forecasting: A machine learning case study in southern Malawi
impact_statement: Forecasts household food insecurity a month ahead so humanitarian agencies can target aid to the most vulnerable families in southern Malawi before shocks hit.
summary: A machine learning study that uses high-frequency household survey data from Catholic Relief Services' MIRA sentinel sites in southern Malawi to identify the drivers of food insecurity and forecast household-level food security in near real-time. A random forest model performed best, with location and self-reported welfare emerging as the strongest predictors, framing the task as binary classification of a food security score.

name: Using internet search trends to forecast short term drug overdose deaths: A case study on Connecticut
impact_statement: Gives state health agencies a way to anticipate weekly spikes in drug overdose deaths using only publicly available data, so interventions can be aimed before fatalities rise.
summary: A forecasting model that combines publicly available historic death records with internet search trends for drug related terms to predict the number of, and spikes in, overdose deaths in the coming week. Demonstrated as a case study in Connecticut, it forecasts overdose fatalities with good accuracy against several baselines without relying on private health records.

name: Evaluating the generalizability of deep learning algorithms to detect middle ear disease using otoscopy
impact_statement: Shows that AI otoscopy tools which detect middle ear disease lose substantial accuracy on patients from new clinics, flagging a gap that must be closed before such tools can be trusted for real world ear health screening.
summary: This study tested how well deep learning image classifiers that flag middle ear disease from otoscopic images generalize across settings, using 1842 images from Turkey, Chile, and the USA. Models scored high on internal data (mean AUC 0.95) but dropped markedly on external images (mean AUC 0.76), while pooling all cohorts recovered strong performance (AUC 0.96).

name: GeoAI Agency Primitives
impact_statement: Defines a shared vocabulary of agency capabilities and a productivity benchmark so AI assistants can measurably speed up the everyday map-making work of GIS practitioners.
summary: Ongoing research proposing nine agency primitives, including navigation, perception, geo-referenced memory, and dual modeling, that connect foundation models to the artifact-centric, human-in-the-loop workflows GIS practitioners actually use. It also introduces a benchmark that measures human productivity, aiming to make agentic assistance in GIS implementable, testable, and comparable.

name: GeoVision Labeler: Zero-Shot Geospatial Classification with Vision and Language Models
impact_statement: Enables classifying satellite imagery in regions where labeled training data is scarce or unavailable, supporting applications like disaster response and land-use monitoring without any task-specific training.
summary: GeoVision Labeler (GVL) is a strictly zero-shot geospatial image classification framework that uses a vision language model to generate rich, human-readable descriptions of satellite imagery and then maps those descriptions to user-defined classes with a conventional language model. The modular, interpretable pipeline reaches up to 93.2% accuracy on a binary buildings task on SpaceNet v7 and uses recursive LLM-driven clustering for hierarchical multi-class classification on UC Merced and RESISC45.

name: GhostNetZero: AI for Detecting Marine Ghost Nets
impact_statement: Flags abandoned fishing nets in sonar imagery so cleanup teams can locate and remove the gear that entangles and kills marine wildlife.
summary: GhostNetZero applies computer vision to side-scan sonar imagery to automatically detect abandoned, lost, or discarded fishing gear (ghost nets), reaching roughly a 90% detection rate in the Baltic Sea and Puget Sound. It pairs the AI predictions with a human-in-the-loop web platform where experts review and validate detections, feeding corrections back to iteratively refine the model.

name: Height Estimation of Children under Five Years using Depth Images
impact_statement: Lets frontline health workers screen young children for stunting and malnutrition from a single smartphone depth scan, without specialized anthropometric equipment in low-resource settings.
summary: A CNN estimates the standing height of children under five from smartphone depth images, targeting the SMART methodology accuracy threshold of 1.4 cm. Trained on 87,131 depth images, it reached a 1.64% mean absolute error and fell within the acceptable range for 70.3% of test images, enabling low-cost detection of stunting (low height-for-age) where scarce resources make manual anthropometry hard.

name: HERITAGE: A Global Multi-Temporal Satellite Dataset for Archaeological Site Monitoring
impact_statement: Enables monitoring of nearly 2,000 archaeological sites across 16 countries to detect looting and disturbance from monthly satellite imagery, helping protect cultural heritage from destruction.
summary: HERITAGE is a global satellite image dataset for monitoring archaeological sites, built from Planet Labs monthly basemap mosaics at 4.77 m/pixel covering 1,982 sites across 16 countries with 113 monthly observations (212,776 images) from January 2016 to May 2025. Its Afghanistan subset provides binary looting labels for 1,943 sites (898 looted, 1,045 preserved) plus per-site bounding masks and month-of-disturbance annotations for confirmed change events. The dataset, with site coordinates withheld for protection, supports training and evaluation of change-detection and looting-classification models for cultural heritage preservation.

name: HLA-genotype-based Predictive Diagnosis of T-cell Responses to SARS-CoV-2 Infection Powered by Machine Learning
impact_statement: Predicts which SARS-CoV-2 epitopes trigger an individual's protective T-cell response from their HLA genotype, enabling personalized assessment of immunity and reinfection risk.
summary: VERDI is a machine learning diagnostic tool that uses a person's six HLA class I alleles to rank SARS-CoV-2 epitopes by their potential to induce T-cell responses. Trained on 920 clinical epitopes and validated against the independent dataset from the FDA-approved T-Detect COVID test, it improved T-cell response prediction recall threefold and precision eightfold over prior HLA-allele-based models.

name: How effective is multifactor authentication at deterring cyberattacks?
impact_statement: Quantifies how much multifactor authentication protects online accounts, showing it keeps over 99.99 percent of enabled accounts secure and cuts compromise risk by more than 99 percent, strengthening the case for turning it on by default.
summary: This study measures how well multifactor authentication (MFA) protects commercial accounts from unauthorized access, using the benchmark-multiplier method and manual review on a large set of Microsoft Azure Active Directory users showing suspicious activity. It finds that MFA reduces the risk of account compromise by about 99 percent overall and by 98.56 percent even when credentials have leaked, with dedicated authenticator apps outperforming SMS codes. The authors recommend enabling MFA by default on commercial accounts.

name: How good are humans at detecting AI-generated images? Learnings from an experiment
impact_statement: Measures how reliably people can tell real photographs from AI-generated ones, quantifying the misinformation risk that makes watermarking and detection tools necessary.
summary: Using roughly 287,000 image judgments from over 12,500 global players of the online "Real or Not Quiz", the study measures how well people distinguish AI-generated images from real ones. Participants scored only 62 percent overall, doing best on human portraits and worst on natural and urban landscapes, underscoring the need for transparency tools like watermarks and robust AI-detection systems.

name: How to Handle Armed Conflict Data in a Real-World Scenario
impact_statement: Helps under-resourced conflict-analysis nonprofits turn messy armed conflict reports into usable structured data with far less manual work and reduced reporting bias.
summary: This work applies a deep natural language processing model to automate the transformation and classification of armed conflict event data, which is otherwise inconsistent, uncertain, and costly to standardize by hand. The model cuts the manual effort nonprofits spend on data cleaning, identifies multiple incident types, and lets practitioners draw on a broader range of conflict sources to reduce reporting bias.

name: Improving Lesion Detection by exploring bias on Skin Lesion dataset
impact_statement: Exposes hidden dataset biases that let melanoma-detection models cheat, guarding against unreliable AI in early skin cancer screening.
summary: This work investigates spurious biases in the widely used ISIC skin lesion archive by training deep learning models on shape-preserving masked images that remove clinically meaningful content. Finding that such models still classify lesions without valid signal, the authors show the models exploit spurious correlations and propose a GAN-based approach to mitigate the underlying bias.

name: Interpretable and Explainable Machine Learning for Materials Science and Chemistry
impact_statement: Helps materials scientists and chemists trust machine learning predictions and surface genuine scientific insights rather than spurious correlations by making models interpretable and explainable.
summary: A review of interpretability and explainability techniques for machine learning applied to materials science and chemistry, showing how they help experts identify model limitations, build trust in predictions, and uncover unexpected correlations. It discusses the challenges of interpretable ML in scientific settings, including the risks of inferring causation and the need for uncertainty estimates on model explanations.

name: Land use and Europe's renewable energy transition: identifying low-conflict areas for wind and solar development
impact_statement: Pinpoints Renewables Acceleration Areas across Europe where wind and solar can scale up to meet REPowerEU targets while sparing the lands most important for biodiversity and rural communities.
summary: A geospatial siting analysis for Europe that estimates how much land wind and solar deployment will require by 2030 and 2050 under business-as-usual versus low-conflict development pathways. By steering renewable expansion toward lower-conflict lands, it identifies acceleration areas that meet energy objectives while minimizing harm to habitats and communities, showing that conventional siting disproportionately targets high-conflict land cover.

name: Modeling to explore and challenge inherent assumptions when cultural norms have changed: a case study on left-handedness and life expectancy
impact_statement: Shows that historical shifts in how left-handedness was reported fully account for the long-cited claim that left-handed people die nine years younger, correcting a widespread public health misconception.
summary: A modeling and simulation study that reexamines the 1991 claim of a nine-year life expectancy gap between left- and right-handed people. Using all US deaths in 1989 by birth year, gender, and handedness alongside counterfactual handedness rates for cohorts born 1900 to 1989, it demonstrates that changing cultural reporting of left-handedness, not any real mortality difference, explains the original finding.

name: Leveraging Large Language Models to Generate Multiple-Choice Questions for Ophthalmology Education
impact_statement: Eases the slow, labor-intensive work of writing ophthalmology board and residency exams by showing GPT-4 can generate training questions rated on par with those from expert human committees.
summary: A survey study testing whether general-domain large language models, particularly GPT-4o, can generate high-quality, novel multiple-choice questions for ophthalmology residency training grounded in the American Academy of Ophthalmology's Basic and Clinical Science Course. Ten expert ophthalmologists, masked to the source, rated AI-generated questions against those written by a human examination committee across appropriateness, clarity, relevance, discriminative power, and suitability for trainees.

name: Liver MRI proton density fat fraction inference from contrast enhanced CT using deep learning
impact_statement: Shows that routine contrast enhanced CT scans can approximate the gold-standard MRI measure of liver fat, opening a path to detect fatty liver disease in patients who lack access to specialized MRI.
summary: A proof-of-concept study that trains a 3D U-Net deep learning model to infer liver MRI proton density fat fraction (PDFF), the most accurate noninvasive measure of liver fat, directly from contrast enhanced CT images. Using paired CT and MRI-PDFF exams from living liver donor candidates, it tests whether more accessible and affordable CT imaging can quantify liver fat for earlier diagnosis of metabolic dysfunction-associated steatotic liver disease.

name: A Methodology for Using Large Language Models to Create User-Friendly Applications for Medicaid Redetermination and Other Social Services
impact_statement: Turns dense state Medicaid eligibility rules into an accessible web tool so people facing redetermination can check whether they still qualify for health coverage.
summary: A methodology that uses large language models to extract Medicaid eligibility rules from publicly available state documents and convert them into a user-friendly web application. Demonstrated for Washington, South Carolina, and North Dakota, it helps beneficiaries assess their eligibility amid the unwinding of Medicaid's continuous enrollment provision, and generalizes to other social services.

name: Local vs. Global: Local Land-Use and Land-Cover Models Deliver Higher Quality Maps
impact_statement: More accurate local land-cover maps help target agricultural monitoring and food security efforts in African regions that global maps map poorly.
summary: A data-centric teacher-student framework that produces high-quality local land-use and land-cover maps for regions underserved by global maps. A high-resolution teacher model (0.331 m/pixel) generates weak labels that train a low-resolution student model on freely available 10 m/pixel satellite imagery, and the approach is evaluated in Murang'a county, Kenya to improve on globally trained maps that are less accurate across Africa.

name: Identifying long-term effects of SARS-CoV-2 and their association with social determinants of health in a cohort of over one million COVID-19 survivors
impact_statement: Reveals which long-term conditions COVID-19 survivors develop and how social factors like race, income, and education shape that risk, informing more equitable post-COVID care.
summary: This study analyzed a US medical claims database of over 1.37 million COVID-19 survivors to identify long-term effects of SARS-CoV-2 infection. Using a self-controlled cohort design, it found 36 diagnostic codes significantly elevated after infection, then used logistic regression to link those effects to social determinants of health such as race, income, education level, and veteran status.

name: MACE: A Flexible Framework for Membership Privacy Estimation in Generative Models
impact_statement: Gives institutions a rigorous way to measure how much a generative model leaks about the individuals in its training data before that model or its synthetic outputs are shared.
summary: MACE is a formal framework for estimating membership privacy risk in generative machine learning models. It frames the risk as a statistical divergence between training and hold-out samples and provides sample-based estimators with theoretical guarantees, offering both population-level and individual-level privacy metrics that work under more realistic assumptions than prior methods, including for imbalanced datasets.

name: Machine Learning for Glacier Monitoring in the Hindu Kush Himalaya
impact_statement: Accelerates ecological monitoring of climate-threatened Himalayan glaciers by letting experts map and correct clean-ice and debris-covered glacier extents from satellite imagery.
summary: A machine learning approach for semi-automated glacier mapping in the Hindu Kush Himalaya, using readily available remote sensing data to identify and outline both clean ice and debris-covered glaciers. The team also releases training data and a web tool that lets domain experts visualize and correct model predictions to speed up glacier mapping.

name: Assessing the utility of machine learning for predicting food sufficiency: a case study in Malawi
impact_statement: Pinpoints which areas of Malawi are most at risk of food insufficiency using only publicly available data, helping direct food assistance toward the communities repeatedly hit by food crises.
summary: This study evaluates machine learning models (TabNet, random forests, and LASSO) alongside logistic regression for predicting household food insufficiency across Malawi's census enumeration areas, combining monthly survey reports with remotely sensed crop-production factors and maize price observations. It finds that simple models perform comparably to complex ones, that maize price is the leading predictor, and that food crises are highly path-dependent, recurring in the same regions over time.

name: A machine learning pipeline for aiding school identification from child trafficking images
impact_statement: Helps law enforcement identify trafficked children faster by automatically spotting school uniforms in intercepted images to narrow down a child's school of origin.
summary: A proof-of-concept computer vision pipeline that detects whether a child in an image is wearing a school uniform and classifies uniform attributes such as color and texture of shirts, sweaters, and blazers. By automating part of the school-of-origin identification that police currently do manually, it aims to speed up child identification in trafficking investigations.

name: Machine learning to evaluate the relationship between social determinants and diabetes prevalence in New York City
impact_statement: Shows New York City policy-makers which neighbourhood social conditions most drive diabetes disparities so they can target local interventions where they matter most.
summary: A cross-sectional study that uses gradient boosting models with Shapley Additive Explanations to quantify how social determinants of health such as socioeconomic position, educational attainment, food access, air quality, housing conditions and insurance coverage relate to diabetes prevalence across 2,096 New York City census tracts. It measures the cumulative contribution of these determinants and identifies the ones most strongly associated with neighbourhood diabetes rates to inform place-based public health strategies.

name: Machine Learning for Sustainable Rice Production: Region-Scale Monitoring of Water-Saving Practices in Punjab, India
impact_statement: Maps where Punjab farmers adopt water-saving rice practices so groundwater conservation and climate adaptation policies can target the fields that need them.
summary: A machine learning framework uses Sentinel-1 satellite imagery to monitor the adoption of water-saving rice farming practices across Punjab, India, at regional scale. A dimensional classification approach decouples sowing method (Direct-Seeded Rice) from irrigation method (Alternate Wetting and Drying), reaching F1 scores of 0.8 and 0.74 respectively, trained on ground-truth data from 1,400 fields collected with agronomy experts and a large-scale farmer training program.

name: Enhanced Macular Telangiectasia Type 2 Detection: Leveraging Self-Supervised Learning and Ensemble Models
impact_statement: Enables accurate, interpretable automated screening for macular telangiectasia type 2, a rare sight-threatening retinal disease, from routine OCT scans at expert-grader level.
summary: This work builds an ensemble of supervised and self-supervised deep learning models to classify macular telangiectasia (MacTel) type 2 versus non-MacTel from OCT retinal scans. Trained on 5,200 images from the MacTel Registry and the University of Washington, the ensemble matches human expert graders and uses Grad-CAM visualizations to make its decisions interpretable, including under limited labeled data.

name: Mapping Glacial Lakes Using Historically Guided Segmentation Models
impact_statement: Enables faster, automated mapping of Himalayan glacial lakes to support outburst flood risk assessments that protect downstream communities and infrastructure.
summary: This work compares segmentation approaches that use labels from a 2015 glacial lake survey as a weak historical prior to map glacial lakes in the Hindu Kush Himalayas from newer Sentinel-2 and Bing Maps imagery. A guided version of U-Net and a properly initialized form of morphological snakes each improve Intersection-over-Union by 8 to 10 percent over existing U-Net approaches, and the study designs visualizations, including an interactive interface, to help identify lakes of potential concern for glacial lake outburst floods.

name: Mapping Industrial Poultry Operations at Scale With Deep Learning and Aerial Imagery
impact_statement: Produces the first nationwide open map of poultry factory farm locations, giving regulators and the public data to monitor air, water, and health risks that were previously undocumented.
summary: Convolutional neural networks are trained on the USDA's 1 m/pixel National Agricultural Imagery Program aerial imagery to detect individual poultry barns, then run over 42 TB of imagery spanning the continental United States. The result is the first national open-source dataset of poultry concentrated animal feeding operations (CAFOs), validated against hand-labeled facilities across ten California counties to help fill gaps in environmental monitoring.

name: Mapping Refugee Camps with AI: A Benchmark Dataset and Baseline Models for Humanitarian Applications
impact_statement: Gives humanitarian responders accurate maps of refugee camp buildings, solar panels, roof materials, and sanitation facilities so they can better plan infrastructure, distribute resources, and respond to disasters.
summary: This work introduces KakumaAerial, an open-source benchmark that pairs high-resolution aerial imagery of the Kakuma and Kalobeyei refugee settlements in Kenya with annotations for buildings, solar panels, roof materials, and sanitation facilities. It provides baseline machine learning models for humanitarian mapping tasks, reaching strong segmentation performance (IoU of 0.848 for buildings and 0.813 for solar panels) on layouts that existing urban footprint datasets fail to represent.

name: Maternal infections in pregnancy and the risk of sudden unexpected infant death in the offspring in the U.S., 2011–2015
impact_statement: Identifies treatable maternal infections during pregnancy as risk markers for sudden unexpected infant death, pointing to prenatal screening and treatment as a lever to help protect newborns.
summary: A U.S. cohort study analyzed as a case-control study across all 19.8 million live births from 2011 to 2015, using CDC linked birth-infant death records to test whether maternal chlamydia, gonorrhea, and hepatitis C infections in pregnancy raise the risk of sudden unexpected infant death (SUID). It found these infections more common among mothers of SUID cases, though associations attenuated after adjusting for socioeconomic and demographic factors, suggesting much of the signal reflects residual confounding.

name: Maternal Obesity and Risk of Sudden Unexpected Infant Death
impact_statement: Establishes maternal obesity as a modifiable risk factor for sudden unexpected infant death, informing prevention guidance that could lower infant mortality.
summary: A US nationwide cohort study using CDC linked birth and infant death records for 2015 through 2019 births to test whether maternal prepregnancy obesity raises the risk of sudden unexpected infant death (SUID). It quantifies the relationship between maternal BMI and SUID and estimates the share of SUID cases attributable to maternal obesity.

name: Modeling protective meningococcal antibody responses and factors influencing antibody persistence following vaccination with MenAfriVac using machine learning
impact_statement: Estimates how long protective immunity lasts after MenAfriVac vaccination to help decide when booster doses are needed across Africa's meningitis belt.
summary: Machine learning models trained on serologic data from PsA-TT (MenAfriVac) clinical trials predict how Neisseria meningitidis serogroup A antibody titers change after vaccination, combining demographic and prior-titer variables into short-term and long-term models. The models estimate the vaccine antibody half-life at about 13.9 years, informing booster-timing decisions for the African meningitis belt.

name: The Microsoft-Northwestern-WITNESS Benchmark for Deepfake Detection
impact_statement: Gives journalists and human rights defenders a rigorous, continuously updated benchmark to measure whether AI deepfake detectors actually hold up against emerging generators and adversarial attacks in high-stakes real-world cases.
summary: The MNW benchmark is an evaluation dataset of more than 50,000 AI-generated images, videos, and audio artifacts, paired with real-world AI-manipulated or suspicious media encountered by journalists and human rights defenders and annotated by experts. It is used to evaluate and improve AI-generated content detection algorithms, is periodically updated to cover new generators and state-of-the-art adversarial attacks, and is restricted to evaluation use rather than training or commercial purposes.

name: Machine learning-based derivation and external validation of a tool to predict death and development of organ failure in hospitalized patients with COVID-19
impact_statement: Gives clinicians a validated risk tool that flags hospitalized COVID-19 patients at high risk of death and organ failure within the first 24 hours of admission, supporting faster triage and goals-of-care decisions.
summary: A retrospective cohort study comparing machine learning algorithms on clinical and laboratory data from the first 24 hours of hospital admission to predict in-hospital mortality, ICU transfer, shock requiring vasopressors, and renal replacement therapy in COVID-19 patients. Mortality models were derived and internally validated on 712 University of Washington patients and externally validated on 345 patients from Tongji Hospital in China.

name: Multi-modal Language Models in Bioacoustics with Zero-shot Transfer
impact_statement: Shows that audio-language models can recognize wildlife sounds without any labeled training examples, opening a path to cheaper annotation-free acoustic monitoring for conservation.
summary: A case study evaluating multi-modal audio-text language models, specifically CLAP, for zero-shot bioacoustic sound event detection across benchmarks spanning birds, frogs, minke whales, meerkats, and gunshots. It probes how well such models generalize to wildlife monitoring tasks without task-specific labeled data, documenting both their potential and their limitations for real-world deployment.

name: Multimodal Foundation Models for Zero-shot Animal Species Recognition in Camera Trap Images
impact_statement: Lets conservationists identify wildlife species in camera trap photos without expert-labeled training data, making large-scale population monitoring cheaper and faster to deploy.
summary: WildMatch is a zero-shot species classification framework that instruction-tunes vision-language models to generate expert-style visual descriptions of camera trap images, then matches those captions against an external knowledge base of species descriptions to identify animals without labeled training data. It uses a knowledge augmentation technique to improve caption quality and was demonstrated on a new camera trap dataset from the Magdalena Medio region of Colombia.

name: New frontiers in artificial intelligence for biodiversity research and conservation with multimodal language models
impact_statement: Opens biodiversity monitoring to practitioners without programming expertise by letting multimodal language models classify species and interpret animal behavior at scale, accelerating conservation.
summary: A review of how multimodal language models can advance biodiversity research and conservation. It shows these models can classify species and recognize complex concepts such as animal posture and orientation without task-specific training, explain their predictions, and interact with people in natural language, and it maps the opportunities and challenges for making such AI tools transparent and accessible to non-specialists.

name: Multispecies bioacoustic classification using transfer learning of deep convolutional neural networks with pseudo-labeling
impact_statement: Enables automated acoustic monitoring of birds and amphibians of conservation concern across Puerto Rico's forests by accurately identifying their calls in continuous field recordings.
summary: The work trains deep convolutional neural networks to classify the calls of 24 bird and amphibian species from ambient field recordings collected across roughly 700 sites in the mountains of Puerto Rico. By combining transfer learning from a pre-trained CNN with a semi-supervised pseudo-labeling method and a custom loss function, it reaches over 95 percent sensitivity and specificity, supporting scalable biodiversity monitoring from passive acoustic sensors.

name: Open Data on GitHub: Unlocking the Potential of AI
impact_statement: Maps the vast landscape of open datasets hosted on GitHub so researchers and organizations can discover and reuse them to accelerate AI work on complex societal problems.
summary: This study analyzes the landscape of open data on GitHub, showing it hosts more than 800 million open data files (142 terabytes) and has grown rapidly over four years. It characterizes how users share datasets and releases three collected datasets to improve discoverability and reuse of open data for AI research.

name: Open Datasheets: Machine-readable Documentation for Open Datasets and Responsible AI Assessments
impact_statement: Lets open-data users quickly discover and vet whether a dataset fits their needs and responsible AI policies before they build on it.
summary: Open Datasheets is a no-code, machine-readable documentation framework for open datasets that foregrounds responsible AI considerations. It standardizes how dataset creators describe content, context, and quality so researchers and data scientists can more easily find, understand, and evaluate datasets against their requirements and regulations.

name: Optimizing Cloud-to-GPU Throughput for Deep Learning With Earth Observation Data
impact_statement: Lets researchers train Earth observation models by streaming imagery straight from cloud storage at the same accuracy and speed as local disk, removing the cost and friction of copying petabyte-scale datasets.
summary: This work benchmarks and tunes how GeoTIFF Earth observation data is streamed from cloud object storage and local SSD into PyTorch training pipelines. Using tile-aligned reads, worker thread pools, and Bayesian optimization, the optimized configurations raise remote loading throughput by 20x and local throughput by 4x, keeping GPUs 85-95% utilized so cloud-based training matches local training accuracy within the same time budget.

name: Overhead MegaDetector - OWL (Overhead Wildlife Locator) Benchmark
impact_statement: Enables reproducible, retraining-free counting of caribou from aerial survey imagery so wildlife managers can monitor Arctic herd populations across different years and regions.
summary: OWL (Overhead Wildlife Locator) is a benchmark for point-based detection and counting of wildlife in overhead aerial imagery, built from point-annotated 512x512 caribou survey patches from two Alaskan herds. It defines a strict cross-herd and cross-temporal test where detectors trained on the Porcupine Caribou Herd (2017) are evaluated on the Central Arctic Herd (2022) with no per-deployment retraining, and ships pretrained HerdNet and OWL model checkpoints that reproduce the reported results.

name: Overhead Wildlife Locator (OWL): Benchmarking Weakly Supervised Learning for Aerial Wildlife Surveys
impact_statement: Lets conservation teams count wildlife from aerial and drone imagery using cheap point labels instead of costly bounding boxes, cutting the time and expense of large-scale animal surveys
summary: OWL is a weakly supervised density-estimation framework for detecting and counting animals in aerial and drone imagery, trained on point-level labels rather than expensive bounding boxes. It offers three model variants tuned for different regimes and sets a new state of the art across five public aerial wildlife datasets, alongside newly released open patch-level caribou survey datasets.

name: PGRID: Power Grid Reconstruction in Informal Developments Using High-Resolution Aerial Imagery
impact_statement: Reconstructs the low and medium voltage electricity network across Kenya's Kakuma and Kalobeyei refugee camps so utilities and humanitarian agencies can plan reliable power access for more than 200,000 displaced residents.
summary: PGRID uses high-resolution aerial imagery to detect utility poles and infer the distribution lines connecting them, reconstructing the electrical grid in informal settlements where no reliable maps exist. It was applied across 84 km² of the Kakuma refugee camp and Kalobeyei integrated settlement in Turkana, Kenya to support electricity access planning for displaced populations.

name: PocketMiner: predicting cryptic pockets from single protein structures
impact_statement: Expands the set of druggable proteins by rapidly pinpointing hidden pockets on structures once considered untargetable, opening new avenues for drug discovery.
summary: PocketMiner is a graph neural network that predicts, from a single static protein structure, which residues are likely to form cryptic pockets that only appear during molecular motion. It identifies these hidden sites with high accuracy (ROC-AUC 0.87) and more than 1,000 times faster than simulation-based methods, and was applied across the human proteome to surface new potential therapeutic targets.

name: Poultry Barn Mapping
impact_statement: Locates industrial poultry barns across the United States from high-resolution aerial imagery, giving regulators and researchers a nationwide view of concentrated animal feeding operations and their environmental footprint.
summary: This dataset supports detecting poultry barns from high-resolution aerial imagery and provides an accompanying set of model-predicted barn locations across the United States. It pairs training labels and NAIP imagery with a deep learning segmentation pipeline to map industrial poultry operations at scale.

name: Poverty rate prediction using multi-modal survey and earth observation data
impact_statement: Sharpens regional poverty rate estimates by fusing satellite imagery with a short survey, helping target social programs to households below the poverty line.
summary: This work combines household living-standards survey questions with visual features extracted from freely available 10m/px Sentinel-2 imagery to estimate whether households fall below the poverty line via a proxy means test. Adding the satellite features lowers the mean poverty rate estimation error, and the authors propose a method to select a compact set of survey questions that complement the image features, validated on a nationally representative test set in Ethiopia.

name: Predictive Models for Kidney Offer Acceptance: Challenges and Strategies
impact_statement: Gives transplant programs and policymakers more accurate kidney offer acceptance predictions to support fairer risk-adjusted program evaluations and allocation policy.
summary: This work benchmarks machine learning models, feature sets, and sampling strategies for predicting whether a kidney offer will be accepted for transplantation, a task made hard by huge offer volumes and heavily imbalanced acceptance versus decline data.
