Comparing models from logistic regression to gradient boosted trees on donor and candidate characteristics, it finds XGBoost with added features and undersampling improves substantially over the logistic regression baseline, establishing baselines and best practices for offer acceptance prediction.

name: privGAN: Protecting GANs from membership inference attacks at low cost to utility
impact_statement: Lets data holders release GAN-generated synthetic images that resist membership inference attacks, keeping individual training records private while preserving the data's usefulness for downstream tasks.
summary: privGAN is a GAN architecture whose generator is trained both to fool the discriminator and to defend against membership inference attacks that try to reveal which records were in the training set. It explicitly prevents memorization of the training data, providing strong empirical privacy protection while keeping synthetic sample quality and downstream task performance close to non-private GANs.

name: Prompt Validation in Large Language Models for Ophthalmology Education: Reply
impact_statement: Clarifies how the survey behind prompt validation was built so large language models can be assessed more reliably as ophthalmology education tools.
summary: A reply in JAMA Ophthalmology responding to correspondence about the authors' earlier study on prompt validation in large language models for ophthalmology education. It lays out the step-by-step process used to construct the validation survey.

name: PteroSet
impact_statement: Provides an expertly annotated acoustic reference dataset that enables automated detection and identification of Neotropical bird species to support biodiversity monitoring in Colombia.
summary: PteroSet is a passive acoustic monitoring dataset of Neotropical bird vocalizations recorded with autonomous AudioMoth devices in Colombia between 2023 and 2025. It comprises 563 recordings totaling 73.62 hours with 15,372 expert bird-event annotations (6,702 with species-level labels), harmonized into a COCO-inspired JSON schema with explicit time and frequency bounds for sound event detection and species classification.

name: PteroSet: A strongly annotated passive acoustic dataset for tropical bird monitoring
impact_statement: Enables scalable, non-invasive monitoring of tropical bird biodiversity by giving machine learning practitioners an expertly labeled benchmark of Neotropical soundscapes for training and evaluating acoustic detection models.
summary: PteroSet is a strongly annotated passive acoustic dataset of Neotropical bird vocalizations recorded in Puerto Asis (Putumayo) and Pivijay (Magdalena), Colombia, comprising 563 recordings (73.6 hours) and 15,372 time-frequency annotations, including 6,702 species-level events across 168 species. It is released in a COCO-inspired JSON schema and paired with a deep learning baseline for bird detection, serving as a realistic benchmark that highlights the challenges of tropical soundscapes such as acoustic co-occurrence and domain shift across sites.

name: Pytorch-Wildlife: A Collaborative Deep Learning Framework for Conservation
impact_statement: Puts automated wildlife detection and species classification within reach of conservationists with little or no coding background, accelerating large-scale biodiversity monitoring.
summary: Pytorch-Wildlife is an open-source, PyTorch-based platform for creating, modifying, and sharing AI models that detect and classify animals in images and videos. It provides an intuitive interface accessible through local installation or Hugging Face, lowering the technical barrier for wildlife monitoring, and has been used to train species recognition models for Amazon wildlife.

name: Novel Data Analytics Identify Predictors of Quality-of-Life Trajectories in Patients with AML or High-Risk Myeloid Neoplasms
impact_statement: Reveals which baseline patient factors predict long-term quality-of-life outcomes for people with acute myeloid leukemia, helping clinicians anticipate decline and guide treatment decisions.
summary: This study applied unsupervised longitudinal clustering and ordinal logistic regression to prospective quality-of-life questionnaires from 503 patients with AML or high-risk myeloid neoplasms enrolled in an observational clinical trial. It identified six distinct quality-of-life trajectories and the baseline factors independently associated with them, finding that intensive therapy was linked to more favorable trajectories even in older or higher-comorbidity patients.

name: Rapid and accurate prediction of protein homo-oligomer symmetry using Seq2Symm
impact_statement: Predicts how proteins assemble into symmetric complexes from a single sequence at roughly 80,000 proteins per hour, enabling proteome-scale insight into protein function that would otherwise require slow all-atom structure modeling.
summary: Seq2Symm fine-tunes protein foundation models (its best variant built on ESM2) to predict the symmetry of homo-oligomeric protein assemblies directly from amino acid sequence. It outperforms template-based and prior deep learning methods on held-out test sets and was applied to five proteomes and about 3.5 million unlabeled sequences to accelerate downstream structure prediction with tools like RoseTTAFold2 and AlphaFold2-multimer.

name: Towards reliable use of AI to classify otitis media using otoscopic images
impact_statement: Exposes hidden dataset biases that undermine AI screening for childhood ear disease, so diagnostic models can generalize across populations and help prevent avoidable hearing loss.
summary: This retrospective study systematically evaluates three public otoscopic image datasets (Chile, Ohio USA, and Türkiye) for biases that limit the reliability of AI models classifying otitis media. Through counterfactual experiments that mask clinically relevant features and alter image hue, saturation, and value, it shows models often achieve high internal accuracy by relying on dataset-specific artifacts rather than clinical signals, and it points to data-quality practices that make ear-disease diagnosis more trustworthy and generalizable.

name: Retinal Microvasculature as Biomarker for Diabetes and Cardiovascular Diseases
impact_statement: Shows that blood vessel patterns in routine retinal photographs can serve as a reliable non-invasive biomarker for detecting diabetic retinopathy and, by extension, screening for cardiovascular disease risk.
summary: This work applies deep learning convolutional neural networks to color fundus images to segment retinal blood vessels and classify diabetic retinopathy severity, using harmonic-descriptor vessel reconstruction to smooth and denoise the vasculature. It finds that most diabetic retinopathy biomarkers are topologically tied to the retinal microvasculature, with vasculature defects explaining 93.8% to 96.7% of referral and sight-threatening classifications.

name: Revisiting pre-trained remote sensing model benchmarks: resizing and normalization matters
impact_statement: Aligning image resizing and normalization with pre-training conventions makes standard ImageNet-pretrained models a competitive, low-cost baseline for satellite and aerial image analysis, lifting downstream accuracy by up to 32 points.
summary: This work evaluates self-supervised and ImageNet pre-trained model embeddings across seven satellite and aerial imagery datasets, showing that simply matching the image resizing and normalization used during pre-training produces large downstream accuracy gains. It finds that with these overlooked preprocessing steps, ImageNet pre-training remains a competitive baseline for remote sensing transfer learning.

name: Risk factors for late (28+ weeks' gestation) stillbirth in the United States, 2014-2015
impact_statement: Identifies the modifiable maternal factors that drive the largest share of late stillbirths in the US, pointing to where prevention efforts can save the most fetal lives.
summary: A retrospective population-based analysis of all singleton US births in 2014-2015 using CDC live birth and fetal death records to quantify risk factors for late (28+ weeks) stillbirth. It highlights modifiable contributors such as high maternal BMI, small-for-gestational-age infants, inadequate prenatal care, diabetes, hypertension, and smoking, alongside sociodemographic disparities.

name: Risk of hospitalization and mortality after breakthrough SARS-CoV-2 infection by vaccine type and previous SARS-CoV-2 infection utilizing medical claims data
impact_statement: Measures how vaccine type and a prior infection change the odds of severe COVID-19 among breakthrough cases, giving clinicians and health officials evidence to guide vaccine and booster decisions.
summary: An epidemiological study of medical claims data for 17,881 breakthrough SARS-CoV-2 infections comparing hospitalization and mortality risk across Pfizer, Moderna, and Janssen vaccine recipients. Using Cox regression it finds lower risk for Moderna and higher risk for Janssen relative to Pfizer, and that a SARS-CoV-2 infection prior to vaccination independently lowered the risk of hospitalization and death across age, sex, comorbidities, and vaccine type.

name: Risks of Using Non-verified Open Data: Predicting Pregnancy Outcomes in India
impact_statement: Warns practitioners that unverified open health data from India can yield misleading pregnancy-outcome predictions, helping guard against flawed AI decisions in resource-poor public health settings.
summary: A case study applying machine learning to an open Indian health dataset to predict pregnancy outcomes. It shows how missing, mislabeled, and improperly collected data, together with a poor understanding of reporting metrics, can lead to erroneous conclusions, and cautions against using non-verified open data for AI in developing-country health settings.

name: The Road to India's Renewable Energy Transition Must Pass through Crowded Lands
impact_statement: Provides land use recommendations that help India deploy 500 GW of renewable energy by 2030 while reducing conflicts over crowded lands and safeguarding socio-ecological values
summary: A perspective and policy analysis of the land use conflicts that stand between India and its target of 500 GW of installed renewable energy by 2030, which would require adding 42 GW every year. It examines the socio-ecological risks of siting new solar and wind infrastructure on already crowded lands and offers a series of recommendations to mainstream land values into renewable energy planning and deployment.

name: Satellite-Based Detection of Looted Archaeological Sites Using Machine Learning
impact_statement: Enables scalable monitoring of thousands of remote archaeological sites from satellite imagery to protect cultural heritage from looting.
summary: A satellite-based machine learning pipeline that detects looted archaeological sites from monthly PlanetScope mosaics, built on a curated dataset of 1,943 sites in Afghanistan with multi-year imagery and site-footprint masks. ImageNet-pretrained CNNs combined with spatial masking reach an F1 score of 0.926, clearly outperforming traditional ML on handcrafted features and geospatial foundation model embeddings.

name: SCOPE: AI-Assisted Early Detection of Potentially Curable Pancreatic Neoplasms on CT from Local and Global Information
impact_statement: Helps radiologists catch small, still-curable pancreatic tumors on CT scans earlier, improving the odds of timely treatment for a cancer usually found too late.
summary: SCOPE (Small-lesion COntextual Pancreatic Evaluator) is a deep learning model that detects small pancreatic lesions on contrast-enhanced CT by combining voxel-level segmentation of the pancreas, ducts, and lesions with global contextual signals in a case-level metamodel. Trained on 4,065 CT scans and evaluated on an internal holdout set, the multi-institutional PANORAMA challenge dataset, and an expert-curated small-lesion reader study, it improves detection of pancreatic ductal adenocarcinoma, neuroendocrine tumors, and cystic lesions over segmentation baselines.

name: Seeing the roads through the trees: A benchmark for modeling spatial dependencies with aerial imagery
impact_statement: Exposes where aerial road-mapping models fail on tree-occluded roads so geospatial maps stay accurate even where canopy hides the ground.
summary: This work introduces the Chesapeake Roads Spatial Context (RSC) benchmark, a road segmentation dataset built from NAIP aerial imagery for measuring how well geospatial machine learning models reason over long-range spatial context. It shows that standard segmentation models degrade sharply under occlusion, for example a U-Net that recalls 84% of visible roads recovers only 63.5% of roads covered by tree canopy.

name: Self-Supervised Learning for Improved Optical Coherence Tomography Detection of Macular Telangiectasia Type 2
impact_statement: Enables accurate detection of the rare retinal disease macular telangiectasia type 2 from OCT scans even when labeled patient data is scarce.
summary: This work develops a self-supervised deep learning approach that pretrains convolutional neural networks on unlabeled optical coherence tomography images using the Bootstrap Your Own Latent algorithm, then fine-tunes on limited labeled data to classify macular telangiectasia type 2. It shows that self-supervised pretraining can outperform fully supervised ResNet models for detecting this rare retinal disease when labeled data is limited.

name: Sims: An interactive tool for geospatial matching and clustering
impact_statement: Lets researchers rapidly discover predictive geospatial features and delineate similar regions, such as maize yield response zones in Rwanda, without heavy computing or coding.
summary: Sims (Similarity Search) is an open-source, no-code web tool that runs clustering and similarity search over user-defined regions of interest using Google Earth Engine as its backend. It focuses on geospatial feature exploration rather than model building, demonstrated through a case study on simulated maize yield response zones in Rwanda across soil, weather, and agronomic features.

name: Social connectedness and movements among communities of giraffes vary by sex and age class
impact_statement: Maps how giraffe social ties and movements differ by sex and age, informing conservation planning for endangered Masai giraffes in Tanzania.
summary: This work applies social network analysis and community detection to five years of sightings of more than 1,000 individually identified wild Masai giraffes in Tanzania's Tarangire Ecosystem. It detects four mixed-sex "super-communities" and shows that adult males and young giraffes are more socially connected and transition between communities more often than adult females, reflecting differences in reproductive and life-history strategies.

name: Association Between Average Annual US Medical Specialty Compensation and Percentage of Trainees in the Specialty Who Are Female
impact_statement: Quantifies how a medical specialty's average compensation tracks with the share of its trainees who are female, giving residency program directors evidence to argue for income and specialty-choice equity for women physicians.
summary: A statistical study relating the average annual compensation of 50 US medical specialties in 2023 to the proportion of trainees in each specialty who are female. Using stepwise linear regression on 2023 graduate medical education trainee data and publicly available Doximity compensation figures, it tests whether higher-paying specialties draw a smaller percentage of women.

name: Survey Protocol Cards for Crop Maps
impact_statement: Guides agricultural survey planners to spend limited budgets on the label checks that most protect crop map accuracy, which underpins food security decisions.
summary: This work models how crop survey design choices flow through cost and label noise to downstream crop mapping accuracy. Using 17 noise functions grounded in the agricultural survey literature and two datasets (EuroCrops in France and a Zambian smallholder dataset), it finds that label verification matters far more than GPS precision, and packages the findings into a programmable protocol card that optimizes survey design under budget constraints.

name: Temporal Cluster Matching for Change Detection of Structures from Satellite Imagery
impact_statement: It reveals when buildings such as poultry barns and solar farms were constructed or demolished from satellite image time series, enabling longitudinal monitoring even when footprint labels exist for only a single date.
summary: Temporal Cluster Matching (TCM) is a general model that detects when a structure was built or removed by comparing spectral patterns inside and outside its footprint across a time series of remotely sensed imagery, using footprint labels observed only once. A heuristic parameter-selection method lets it be applied to new settings without labeling effort, demonstrated on poultry barns in the Delmarva Peninsula and solar farms in Sentinel-2 mosaics.

name: Testing the Ability of Convolutional Neural Networks to Learn Radiomic Features
impact_statement: Clarifies which tumor characteristics deep learning can reliably measure from cancer PET scans, guiding safer use of AI-derived imaging biomarkers in clinical oncology.
summary: The study trains 2D and 3D convolutional neural networks to predict 16 standardized radiomic features, describing tumor intensity, shape, and texture, from real and synthetic PET images of lymphoma and head and neck lesions. It finds that CNNs learn first-order intensity and size features well but struggle to capture shape irregularity and texture heterogeneity, informing how much trust to place in learned versus hand-crafted imaging biomarkers.

name: The New Digital Divide
impact_statement: Reveals that gaps in how US households actually use their devices persist far beyond broadband access, pointing policy toward digital skills and literacy rather than infrastructure alone.
summary: Using telemetry from operating system updates across forty million Windows devices in US households, this work builds comprehensive new metrics of digital usage at the ZIP code level. It documents an urban-rural divide and large within-metro disparities correlated with income and education, and releases the indices publicly to support future research on digital inequality.

name: Unsupervised background removal by dual-modality PET/CT guidance: application to PSMA imaging of metastases
impact_statement: Makes prostate cancer metastases stand out on PSMA PET scans without needing hand-labeled lesions, supporting easier detection of spreading disease.
summary: A deep learning framework that learns consistent physiological PSMA tracer uptake patterns from paired PET/CT images and subtracts this healthy background, so that metastatic cancer lesions become more prominent in the residual images. Because the approach is unsupervised, it needs no existing lesion segmentations and can use lesion-negative scans, avoiding the large annotated datasets that supervised methods require.

name: U.S. Broadband Coverage Data Set: A Differentially Private Data Release
impact_statement: It gives researchers and policymakers zip-code-level broadband coverage figures to target the digital divide while mathematically protecting the privacy of individual households.
summary: This work releases a publicly available U.S. Broadband Coverage data set reporting broadband coverage percentages at the zip-code level, using differential privacy to guarantee that no individual household's data is disclosed. It also publishes per-zip-code error range estimates that quantify the noise introduced by differential privacy without incurring additional privacy loss.

name: Using Data Science and a Health Equity Lens to Identify Long-COVID Sequelae Among Medically Underserved Populations
impact_statement: Reveals which long-term COVID-19 symptoms burden medically underserved patients so safety-net providers and policymakers can better target their care.
summary: A causal-impact analysis of community health center electronic health records comparing patients who tested positive (n=4,091) versus negative (n=7,118) for acute COVID-19. It identifies 18 statistically significant long-COVID sequelae spanning most organ systems, including breathing abnormalities, malaise and fatigue, and headache, in a large underserved population.

name: Using Website Referrals to Identify Unreliable Content Rabbit Holes
impact_statement: Shows that site-level incentives, not just algorithmic recommenders, trap users in unreliable-content rabbit holes, pointing regulators and platforms toward the economic drivers of misinformation exposure.
summary: This study uses browser telemetry capturing referrals across domains rated as reliable or unreliable information sources to test whether the URL referral structure of websites pulls users into rabbit holes of unreliable content. It finds that unreliable sites strongly retain and re-refer users within their own pages and outperform reliable sites at keeping visitors, though travel between separate unreliable sites is uncommon, implicating financial site-level incentives more than political ones.

name: Weak Labeling for Cropland Mapping in Africa
impact_statement: Produces accurate cropland maps across Africa from almost no hand labeling, helping agricultural planning and food security efforts reach regions where high-resolution maps are scarce.
summary: This work refines weak labels from existing global cropland maps using unsupervised object clustering, then combines them with sparse human annotations to train a semantic segmentation model that identifies cropland. Using only 33 human-annotated labels, adding mined negative labels raised the cropland F1 score from 0.53 to 0.84.

name: Where there's Smoke, there's Fire: Wildfire Risk Predictive Modeling via Historical Climate Data
impact_statement: Gives fire management agencies reliable spatio-temporal forecasts of where wildfires are likely to occur so they can act early to prevent, detect, and suppress them.
summary: A machine learning study that predicts the spatio-temporal likelihood of wildfires from historical burned-area, climate, and geospatial data across three large landscapes in India. It introduces a rigorous temporal evaluation to test which models stay reliable across different locations and time steps for practical deployment.

name: Zero-Shot Transfer for Wildlife Bioacoustics Detection
impact_statement: Lets conservationists identify wildlife sounds like birds, frogs, and whales without the costly manual annotation that limits acoustic monitoring, extending detection to species and habitats never seen during training.
summary: This work applies Contrastive Language-Audio Pretraining (CLAP), a multi-modal model that pairs audio with descriptive text prompts, to wildlife bioacoustics. Without any task-specific fine-tuning, an off-the-shelf CLAP model performs zero-shot transfer across nine bioacoustics benchmarks, matching or beating supervised baselines and enabling flexible class definitions and detection of previously unencountered sounds.
